title: Marsala Marinated Russian Beef Dumplings
description: A cultured Russian dish featuring marsala wine and shio koji cure, wrapped in pandan leaves for a unique flavor and presentation, this halal friendly recipe is perfect for special occasions. The combination of marsala wine and shio koji cure gives the beef a rich and savory taste.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, marsala wine, shio koji, chopped onion, and minced garlic, and mix well until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to cook, preheat a large steamer basket over boiling water.
Lay a pandan leaf flat on a work surface and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the leaf over the filling to form a triangle or a square shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Repeat with the remaining beef mixture and pandan leaves.
Place the dumplings in the steamer basket, leaving some space between each dumpling.
Steam the dumplings over boiling water for 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the dumplings hot with your choice of dipping sauce.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
